scope_and_constraints.objective: Implement JWT authentication middleware in the Express API to secure protected endpoints and validate user tokens
affected_files: src/middleware/auth.js: Create new JWT authentication middleware
src/routes/protected.js: Add authentication to protected routes
tests/middleware/auth.test.js: Add comprehensive JWT middleware tests
constraints: MUST use jsonwebtoken library (already in package.json)
MUST NOT expose private signing keys in code
MUST handle all error cases (missing token, invalid signature, expired token)
MUST NOT break existing API contracts

phase_definitions.objective: Implement JWT authentication middleware and apply it to protected routes
expected_outcomes: Authentication middleware correctly validates JWT tokens
Protected routes reject requests without valid tokens
All existing tests continue to pass
failure_stop_conditions: Code contains TODO, FIXME, or stub implementations
Token validation logic missing or incomplete

verification_gates: GATE_SYNTAX: All JavaScript files parse without syntax errors
GATE_LINT: ESLint passes with zero warnings on auth middleware
GATE_COVERAGE: Auth module has minimum 90% code coverage
forbidden_actions: MUST NOT modify files outside path_allowlist
MUST NOT write stub code (TODO, FIXME, mock implementations)
MUST NOT hardcode secrets or private keys
MUST NOT use insecure token validation
MUST NOT modify database schema or migrations

rollback_procedure: git checkout HEAD -- src/middleware/ src/routes/protected.js tests/middleware/ docs/API_AUTH.md
Delete any newly created files in src/middleware/ or tests/middleware/
Verify workspace state: git status should show clean working tree
Run npm test to confirm workspace is stable
recovery_steps: Review audit-log.jsonl to identify which step failed
Identify the root cause of failure
Fix the issue in the code or plan
Ensure no TODO or stub code remains
Modify plan if needed and resubmit to ANTIGRAVITY
ANTIGRAVITY creates new plan, WINDSURF executes fresh
